rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dithiocyanation of alkynes and alkenes with NH4SCN to give vicinal bis(thiocyanato) vinyl/alkyl arenes
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of SCN- to thiocyanogen radical (•SCN, in equilibrium with (SCN)2); cathodic reduction of TFA protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: β-thiocyanato vinyl/alkyl carbon radical (intermediate A from alkene/alkyne addition of •SCN)
β-thiocyanato vinyl/alkyl cation (intermediate B, from further anodic oxidation of A)

electron_flow: SCN- is oxidized at the anode to •SCN, which adds to the alkyne/alkene to give a carbon-centered radical (A); Path A: a second •SCN couples with A to give the bis-thiocyanate; Path B: A is further oxidized at the anode to a vinyl/alkyl cation (B) that is trapped by SCN-. At the cathode, TFA is reduced to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of 1a suppressed to trace 2a; TEMPO-SCN/vinyl adduct detected by HRMS [M+H]+ = 317.1682. For styrene 3a, 4a yield dropped to 16%.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 2a reduced to 23%; 4a only trace, supporting radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry (NH4SCN, 1a, TFA in MeCN/DCM)
probe_reagents.observation: Oxidation wave observed for NH4SCN; mixture with 1a+NH4SCN+TFA+MeCN+DCM shows enhanced current consistent with anodic SCN- oxidation initiating the reaction

notes: Authors propose two parallel pathways from radical intermediate A: Path A (radical-radical coupling with a second •SCN) is presented alongside Path B (radical-polar crossover via further anodic oxidation to vinyl cation B, then SCN- attack). The paper does not definitively distinguish A vs B. A derivatization experiment (2a + Ph2P(O)H/DBU → 6a) demonstrates synthetic utility but is not mechanistically diagnostic.
